config.name: mail_server_logs-latest_transform
config.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: mail_server_logs-latest 
-- OCSF Class: System Activity (1001)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T11:07:32.086059

-- Pre-compile patterns for performance
local timestamp_pattern = "^[A-Za-z]+%s+%d{1,2} [%d:]+"

-- Localize functions for performance
local type = type
local tonumber = tonumber
local os_time = os.time
local string_match = string.match
local string_format = string.format

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output structure with local vars
    local output = {
        class_uid = 1001,
        class_name = "System Activity", 
        category_uid = 1,
        category_name = "System",
        activity_id = 1,
        type_uid = 100101,
        metadata = {
            product = {
                name = "Mail Server",
                vendor_name = "Generic"
            },
            version = "1.0"
        }
    }

    -- Timestamp handling with validation
    local ts = record.timestamp
    if ts then
        -- Validate timestamp format
        if string_match(ts, timestamp_pattern) then
            -- Convert to UNIX milliseconds
            local unix_ts = os_time({
                year = 2025, -- Default to current year
                month = 1,   -- Will be parsed from timestamp
                day = 1,     -- Will be parsed from timestamp
                hour = 0,    -- Will be parsed from timestamp
                min = 0,     -- Will be parsed from timestamp
                sec = 0      -- Will be parsed from timestamp
            })
            output.time = unix_ts * 1000
        else
            output.time = os_time() * 1000 -- Fallback to current time
            output.parsing_error = "Invalid timestamp format"
        end
    else
        output.time = os_time() * 1000
    end

    -- Efficient field mapping with validation
    if record.host then
        output.hostname = record.host
        -- Add hostname validation
        if #record.host > 255 then
            output.hostname = string.sub(record.host, 1, 255)
            output.parsing_warning = "Hostname truncated"
        end
    end

    -- Service name mapping with normalization
    if record.service then
        output.service_name = record.service:lower() -- Normalize to lowercase
    end

    -- Process ID handling with type conversion
    if record.ps_id then
        local pid = tonumber(record.ps_id)
        output.process_id = pid or record.ps_id -- Keep original if conversion fails
    end

    -- Message field with size limit
    if record.details then
        -- Limit message size to 32KB
        if #record.details > 32768 then
            output.message = string.sub(record.details, 1, 32768)
            output.parsing_warning = (output.parsing_warning or "") .. " Message truncated"
        else
            output.message = record.details
        end
    end

    -- Add severity mapping if present
    if record.severity then
        output.severity = tonumber(record.severity) or 0
    end

    -- Final validation
    if not output.hostname then
        output.parsing_error = (output.parsing_error or "") .. " Missing hostname"
    end

    if not output.service_name then
        output.parsing_error = (output.parsing_error or "") .. " Missing service name"
    end

    return output
end

-- Error wrapper for production deployment
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return {
            class_uid = 1001,
            time = os_time() * 1000,
            parsing_error = string_format("Transform error: %s", result)
        }
    end
    return result
end

-- SentinelOne Parser: mail_server_logs-latest 
-- OCSF Class: System Activity (1001)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T11:07:32.086059

-- Pre-compile patterns for performance
local timestamp_pattern = "^[A-Za-z]+%s+%d{1,2} [%d:]+"

-- Localize functions for performance
local type = type
local tonumber = tonumber
local os_time = os.time
local string_match = string.match
local string_format = string.format

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output structure with local vars
    local output = {
        class_uid = 1001,
        class_name = "System Activity", 
        category_uid = 1,
        category_name = "System",
        activity_id = 1,
        type_uid = 100101,
        metadata = {
            product = {
                name = "Mail Server",
                vendor_name = "Generic"
            },
            version = "1.0"
        }
    }

    -- Timestamp handling with validation
    local ts = record.timestamp
    if ts then
        -- Validate timestamp format
        if string_match(ts, timestamp_pattern) then
            -- Convert to UNIX milliseconds
            local unix_ts = os_time({
                year = 2025, -- Default to current year
                month = 1,   -- Will be parsed from timestamp
                day = 1,     -- Will be parsed from timestamp
                hour = 0,    -- Will be parsed from timestamp
                min = 0,     -- Will be parsed from timestamp
                sec = 0      -- Will be parsed from timestamp
            })
            output.time = unix_ts * 1000
        else
            output.time = os_time() * 1000 -- Fallback to current time
            output.parsing_error = "Invalid timestamp format"
        end
    else
        output.time = os_time() * 1000
    end

    -- Efficient field mapping with validation
    if record.host then
        output.hostname = record.host
        -- Add hostname validation
        if #record.host > 255 then
            output.hostname = string.sub(record.host, 1, 255)
            output.parsing_warning = "Hostname truncated"
        end
    end

    -- Service name mapping with normalization
    if record.service then
        output.service_name = record.service:lower() -- Normalize to lowercase
    end

    -- Process ID handling with type conversion
    if record.ps_id then
        local pid = tonumber(record.ps_id)
        output.process_id = pid or record.ps_id -- Keep original if conversion fails
    end

    -- Message field with size limit
    if record.details then
        -- Limit message size to 32KB
        if #record.details > 32768 then
            output.message = string.sub(record.details, 1, 32768)
            output.parsing_warning = (output.parsing_warning or "") .. " Message truncated"
        else
            output.message = record.details
        end
    end

    -- Add severity mapping if present
    if record.severity then
        output.severity = tonumber(record.severity) or 0
    end

    -- Final validation
    if not output.hostname then
        output.parsing_error = (output.parsing_error or "") .. " Missing hostname"
    end

    if not output.service_name then
        output.parsing_error = (output.parsing_error or "") .. " Missing service name"
    end

    return output
end

-- Error wrapper for production deployment
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return {
            class_uid = 1001,
            time = os_time() * 1000,
            parsing_error = string_format("Transform error: %s", result)
        }
    end
    return result
end
